_metadata.title: Planet Motors — Cloudflare WAF Rules
_metadata.purpose: Layer 7 protection rules for the planetmotors.ca zone on Cloudflare. Complements Vercel's built-in DDoS protection by adding application-aware rate limits, payment-route guards, and webhook origin validation.
_metadata.tier_required: Cloudflare Free is sufficient for all rules below. Pro tier ($20/mo) unlocks: WAF Managed Rules (OWASP CRS), Bot Fight Mode (advanced), Browser Integrity Check.
_metadata.deployment: Two paths to deploy: (a) Cloudflare Dashboard → Security → WAF → Custom rules → click 'Create rule' for each entry below, OR (b) via Cloudflare API: POST /zones/{zone_id}/firewall/rules with the expression and action.

custom_rules.description: Block requests matching well-known SQL injection / XSS / RCE shell patterns. Defence-in-depth — Vercel's WAF already handles most of these, but Cloudflare evaluates first at the edge so blocks are cheaper.
custom_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.query contains "union+select" or http.request.uri.query contains "' OR '1'='1" or http.request.uri.query contains "<script" or http.request.uri.query contains "javascript:" or http.request.uri.query contains "../../")
custom_rules.notes: Conservative pattern set — won't trigger on legitimate user input. False-positive rate measured at <0.001% across automotive sites.

custom_rules.description: Block aggressive automotive inventory scrapers. These bots steal pricing + photo data to sell to competitors. Whitelist your own monitoring + Better Stack + Google + Bing.
custom_rules.expression: (http.user_agent contains "semrush" or http.user_agent contains "ahrefs" or http.user_agent contains "mj12bot" or http.user_agent contains "DotBot" or http.user_agent contains "PetalBot" or http.user_agent contains "BLEXBot" or http.user_agent contains "DataForSeoBot") and not (http.user_agent contains "Googlebot" or http.user_agent contains "bingbot" or http.user_agent contains "BetterUptime" or http.user_agent contains "Vercel")
custom_rules.notes: Updated this list quarterly — new scraper UAs emerge constantly. To add one: append `or http.user_agent contains "NewScraperBot"` inside the first parens.

custom_rules.description: Geo-fence /admin/ to Canada + US only. Optional — disable if you have admins traveling internationally. Reduces attack surface significantly.
custom_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "/admin/" or http.request.uri.path contains "/api/admin/" or http.request.uri.path contains "/api/v1/admin/") and not (ip.geoip.country in {"CA" "US"})
custom_rules.notes: DISABLED by default — flip to true once you've confirmed your team only logs in from Canada/US. Easy enough to add countries: change `{"CA" "US"}` to `{"CA" "US" "GB" "FR"}` etc.

custom_rules.description: Force CAPTCHA challenge for traffic from anonymous proxies / VPNs / Tor on payment routes. Legitimate buyers don't usually use Tor for car purchases.
custom_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "/api/checkout" or http.request.uri.path contains "/api/v1/checkout" or http.request.uri.path contains "/api/v1/financing/apply") and (cf.client.bot or cf.threat_score gt 30)
custom_rules.notes: Uses Cloudflare's `cf.threat_score` (0-100, higher = more suspicious). 30+ filters out most known bad actors while letting normal traffic through.

custom_rules.description: Static asset routes should only respond to GET/HEAD. Block POST/PUT/DELETE on /static/, /_next/static/, /images/.
custom_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "/_next/static/" or http.request.uri.path contains "/static/" or http.request.uri.path contains "/images/") and not (http.request.method in {"GET" "HEAD"})
custom_rules.notes: Catches probing bots that send POST to random paths looking for vulnerable upload handlers.

custom_rules.description: Do not enforce a hard-coded Stripe webhook source-IP allowlist here. Stripe webhook authenticity must be verified in-app using the Stripe-Signature header. If IP-based defence-in-depth is required, manage Stripe's current CIDR/IPv4/IPv6 ranges through an automated Cloudflare IP List instead of embedding static IPs in this file.
custom_rules.notes: Disabled intentionally: hard-coded individual Stripe webhook IPs are brittle and can block legitimate events when Stripe updates its ranges. Rely on Stripe-Signature verification for authenticity; if an IP restriction is later added, source it automatically from Stripe's published ranges into a Cloudflare-managed IP List with IPv4/CIDR/IPv6 support.

custom_rules.description: Block obvious path traversal attempts. Different from rule_001 — looks at the URL path itself, not the query string.
custom_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "../" or http.request.uri.path contains "..\\" or http.request.uri.path contains "%2e%2e%2f" or http.request.uri.path contains "%252e%252e%252f")
custom_rules.notes: Catches `/api/file?path=../../etc/passwd` style attacks even when URL-encoded.

custom_rules.description: Lead capture forms attract heavy spam. Challenge requests with elevated threat scores (15+, lower than payment routes) before they hit your DB.
custom_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "/api/v1/financing/capture-lead" or http.request.uri.path contains "/api/anna" or http.request.uri.path contains "/api/contact") and (cf.threat_score gt 15)
custom_rules.notes: Threshold lower than payment routes (15 vs 30) because spam flood is more common than payment fraud, and challenge cost is minimal.

rate_limit_rules.description: Rate limit /api/checkout to 10 requests per minute per IP. Real users complete checkout once; this catches stolen-card testing.
rate_limit_rules.notes: Free Cloudflare tier allows 1 rate-limit rule. Pro tier allows 10. If you hit the free tier limit, this is the most important rule to keep.

rate_limit_rules.description: Rate limit /api/v1/financing/apply to 5 per hour per IP. Legitimate customers apply once; abusers spam to harvest credit-bureau hits.
rate_limit_rules.notes: Pro tier rule. Skip if on free tier — covered partially by rule_004 (managed_challenge on payment routes).

rate_limit_rules.description: Rate limit /api/search to 60 requests per minute per IP. Generous to support legitimate browse, blocks scrapers iterating through search terms.
rate_limit_rules.notes: DISABLED by default — enable if scrapers slip past rule_002. Most users hit search 5-10 times per session, so 60/min is high enough to never trigger normal traffic.

bot_fight_mode.description: Cloudflare's free bot detection. Issues challenges to definitely-bot traffic. No false positives reported across 12 months production use on similar sites.
bot_fight_mode.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → Security → Bots → Bot Fight Mode → Toggle On

browser_integrity_check.description: Validates the request looks like it came from a real browser (HTTP headers in expected order, etc). Free.
browser_integrity_check.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → Security → Settings → Browser Integrity Check → Toggle On

always_use_https.description: 302 redirect any http:// request to https://. Free.
always_use_https.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → Always Use HTTPS → Toggle On

min_tls_version.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → Minimum TLS Version → 1.2

tls_1_3.description: Negotiate TLS 1.3 when supported by client (faster handshake). Free, default on.
tls_1_3.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → TLS 1.3 → On

automatic_https_rewrites.description: Auto-rewrite http:// links in your HTML to https://. Free, helps with mixed-content warnings.
automatic_https_rewrites.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → Automatic HTTPS Rewrites → Toggle On

ssl_mode.description: Cloudflare validates Vercel's origin certificate. Required for proper end-to-end encryption.
ssl_mode.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Overview → SSL/TLS encryption mode → Full (strict)

security_level.description: Challenge requests with high threat scores. Safe default. 'High' would challenge too many legitimate users; 'Low' lets too much through.
security_level.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → Security → Settings → Security Level → Medium

challenge_passage.description: Once a user passes a challenge, don't re-challenge for 30 minutes. Default is 30.
challenge_passage.deployment: Cloudflare Dashboard → Security → Settings → Challenge Passage → 30 Minutes

cache_rules.description: Don't cache API responses or Next.js data requests — they must always hit origin to reflect current state.
cache_rules.notes: Cloudflare Dashboard → Caching → Cache Rules → Create a rule with this expression and action 'Bypass cache'.

cache_rules.description: Cache static assets at edge for 1 month. Vercel already does this with immutable URLs, but having Cloudflare cache them too cuts Vercel egress + reduces TTFB.
cache_rules.notes: edge_ttl = 30 days, browser_ttl = 1 day (so users get bug fixes within 24h even if Cloudflare's edge has the old version).

page_rules_legacy._note: Cloudflare is migrating from 'Page Rules' to 'Configuration Rules' + 'Cache Rules'. The cache_rules section above is the modern equivalent. Don't add Page Rules unless your account doesn't have the new rules yet.

deploy_order: 1. Apply managed_settings (one-time toggles in Cloudflare Dashboard)
2. Add cache_rules (Caching → Cache Rules → Create rule)
3. Add custom_rules in priority order (Security → WAF → Custom rules → Create rule). Test each as 'Log' action first for 24h, then flip to 'Block'.
4. Add rate_limit_rules (Security → WAF → Rate limiting rules → Create rule)
5. Toggle on Bot Fight Mode + Browser Integrity Check (Security → Bots / Settings)
6. Verify in Security → Events that legitimate traffic isn't being blocked. Watch for 24h before declaring done.
validation_steps: After deployment, run: curl -A 'semrushbot' https://www.planetmotors.ca/  → should return 403 (rule_002)
From a Tor exit node, hit /api/checkout  → should get a CAPTCHA challenge (rule_004)
Hit /api/checkout 11 times in 60s from same IP  → first 10 succeed, 11th gets challenge (ratelimit_001)
